title: cineraria longifolia subsp. arvernensis
intro: Cineraria longifolia subsp. arvernensis, also known as Cineraria arvernensis or Rock cineraria, is a plant species in the daisy family (Asteraceae) that is native to the mountainous regions of Europe. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which make it useful for treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Cineraria arvernensis include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and protect against infection. Cineraria arvernensis can be prepared for medicinal use through herbal infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to fully understand its potential benefits and optimal preparation methods.
